title: UPS GB
tagline: Tool-first home: tracker leads the first viewport, marketing folds below. Brand-faithful refresh at packed density (48px).

direction.phrase: Direction A — tool-first home at density tier C (packed, 48px desktop). Brand-faithful (Mode A, signal-strong).
movements: IA priority: marketing-first hero → tool-first hero (tracker leads)
CTA vocabulary: fragmented → canonical (Track / Get a quote / Start shipping / Log in)
type scale: ad-hoc → modular ratio ~1.25 anchored on body 16px
alt-text floor: 60% empty → 0% accidental empty above the fold
<h1> presence: empty → display H1 above the tracking strip

fontDeck.name: brand-inherited
fontDeck.value: Roboto (heading + body); IBM Plex Sans scoped to /healthcare/ only

palette.name: UPS brand-pinned
brandFaithfulInversions: UPS gold + warm-grey + alert + healthcare-blue retained

originalCritique: The captured home is marketing-first with the tracker buried mid-page, despite return-visitor traffic dominated by tracking-number lookups. The IA fights the audience. Compounding this: empty H1, ~60% empty alt above the fold, 8 distinct radius values, ad-hoc type scale, and a fragmented CTA vocabulary (Get Started + Start Shipping + Ship & Save) that obscures the four canonical tasks.

variants.name: Tool-first home
variants.thesis: Invert the dominant first-viewport surface: the tracker leads, marketing folds below. Brand-faithful palette, type, and logo retained. Density packs to 48px desktop. CTA vocabulary canonicalises to four task verbs.
variants.fix: The tracker leads the first viewport. The captured `` form becomes the hero, with a display H1 above it for SEO/a11y/brand-statement floor. Marketing surfaces fold below the workhorse. Four canonical CTAs (Track / Get a quote / Start shipping / Log in) replace the fragmented Get Started / Start Shipping / Ship & Save trio. Radius vocabulary collapses from 8 values to 3 (4 / 8 / 50%); type scale snaps to a modular 1.25 ratio anchored on body 16px. Crisis affordance preserved as a header-pinned chip + drawer.
